---
title: "FaeSeq-E745 - Annotation Refinement Analysis"
author: "Victor Cornejo Villanueva"
date: "2026-05-20"
output: html_document
---

# Load the package
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

# 1. Read the EggNOG annotation file
```{r}
# EggNOG metadata starts with '##', and the header starts with '#'
file_path <- "e745_functional.emapper.annotations"

# Read the file, skipping the double-hash metadata lines
eggnog_data <- read_tsv(file_path, comment = "##", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Clean the first column name (remove the '#')
colnames(eggnog_data)[1] <- "query"
```

# 2. Process the COG Categories
```{r}
# EggNOG sometimes assigns multiple COGs to one gene (e.g., "KTL"). 
# I split them so each letter gets counted individually
cog_counts <- eggnog_data %>%
  select(query, COG_category) %>%
  # Remove rows with no COG assignment
  filter(!is.na(COG_category) & COG_category != "-") %>%
  # Split multi-letter COGs into separate rows
  separate_rows(COG_category, sep = "") %>%
  # Remove empty strings created by the split
  filter(COG_category != "") %>%
  # Count the frequencies
  count(COG_category, name = "gene_count") %>%
  # Sort from highest to lowest
  arrange(desc(gene_count))
```

# 3. Create the Functional Categorization Plot
```{r}
ggplot(cog_counts, aes(x = reorder(COG_category, -gene_count), y = gene_count, fill = COG_category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  # Add the exact number on top of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = gene_count), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Functional Categorization (COG Distribution)",
    subtitle = "EggNOG-mapper genomic annotations for clinical strain",
    x = "COG Category",
    y = "Number of Genes"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )
```

# 4. Process the categories and extract the gene names
```{r}
eggnog_table <- eggnog_data %>%
  # Remove rows without a category
  filter(!is.na(COG_category) & COG_category != "-") %>%
  # Split multiple letters (e.g., "VM" -> "V", "M")
  separate_rows(COG_category, sep = "") %>%
  filter(COG_category != "") %>%
  # Group by each COG letter
  group_by(COG_category) %>%
  summarise(
    Gene_Count = n(),
    # Extract up to 10 real gene names (Preferred_name column) ignoring empty ones
    My_Main_Genes = paste(head(unique(Preferred_name[!is.na(Preferred_name) & Preferred_name != "-"]), 10), collapse = ", ")
  ) %>%
  # Sort from highest to lowest gene count
  arrange(desc(Gene_Count))

# 3. Save the table in a file
write_csv(eggnog_table, "COG_Table.csv")

print(eggnog_table)
```
